records.ety: [OE. coler, F. colre anger, L. cholera a bilious complaint, fr. Gr. <?/ cholera, fr. <?/, cholh`, bile. See Gall, and cf. Cholera.]

defs: The bile; -- formerly supposed to be the seat and cause of irascibility.
quotes: His [Richard Hooker's] complexion . . . was sanguine, with a mixture of choler; and yet his motion was slow.

defs: Irritation of the passions; anger; wrath.
quotes: He is rash and very sudden in choler.
